reasoning: The BAA10Y spread is currently at 1.65 as of Oct 24, 2025. This value represents a recent local minimum, down from 1.75 in September and 1.92 in April, though still higher than the 1.40s seen in late 2024/early 2025. Because 1.65 is below the recent trend line (~1.68), there is a slightly higher than 50% probability of a short-term bounce-back or mean reversion (FRED linear model estimates ~70% chance of being >1.65 in 1 week). However, strong recent downward momentum (dropping from 1.72 on Oct 10 to 1.65 on Oct 24) and seasonal trends (spreads often tighten into year-end) provide counter-pressure. Following standard random walk principles for asset spreads, uncertainty increases over longer horizons. Therefore, the probability estimates start slightly above 0.5 for the near term due to the expected bounce off the local floor, and then converge steadily toward 0.5 for the more distant dates as variance dominates.
